author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
When the Truth Must Be Spoken, When Kept Back.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: When the Truth Must Be Spoken, When Kept Back.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore let the truth be spoken, especially when any question impels us to declare it; and let them receive it who are able, lest, perchance, while we are silent on account of those who cannot receive it, they be not only defrauded of the truth but be taken captive by falsehood, who are able to receive the truth whereby falsehood may be avoided.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For it is easy, nay, and it is useful, that some truth should be kept back because of those who are incapable of apprehending it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For whence is that word of our Lord: “I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And that of the apostle: “I could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal: as if unto babes in Christ I have given you to drink milk, and not meat, for hitherto ye were not able, neither yet indeed now are ye able”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Although, in a certain manner of speaking, it might happen that what is said should be both milk to infants and meat for grown-up persons.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: As “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” what Christian can keep it back?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Or what in sound doctrine can be found more comprehensive?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And yet this is not kept back either from infants or from grown-up people, nor is it hidden from infants by those who are mature.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But the reason of keeping back the truth is one, the necessity of speaking the truth is another.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It would be a tedious business to inquire into or to put down all the reasons for keeping back the truth; of which, nevertheless, there is this one,—lest we should make those who do not understand worse, while wishing to make those who do understand more learned; although these latter do not become more learned when we withhold any such thing on the one hand, but also do not become worse.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: When, however, a truth is of such a nature that he who cannot receive it is made worse by our speaking it, and he who can receive it is made worse by our silence concerning it, what do we think is to be done?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Must we not speak the truth, that he who can receive it may receive it, rather than keep silence, so that not only neither may receive it, but that even he who is more intelligent should himself be made worse?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For if he should hear and receive it, by his means also many might learn.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For in proportion as he is more capable of learning, he is the more fitted for teaching others.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: The enemy of grace presses on and urges in all ways to make us believe that grace is given according to our deservings, and thus grace is no more grace; and are we unwilling to say what we can say by the testimony of Scripture?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Do we fear, forsooth, to offend by our speaking him who is not able to receive the truth? and are we not afraid lest by our silence he who can receive the truth may be involved in falsehood?
